items.title: Type-check separately
items.avoid: Relying on the bundler for type safety.
items.prefer: A dedicated tsc --noEmit step in CI.

items.title: Favor static ESM imports
items.prefer: Named imports of what you use.
items.example: `import { Button } from '@/ui/Button'` (named) lets the bundler drop other UI components. `import * as UI from '@/ui'` (namespace) keeps them all.

items.title: Start simple
items.prefer: Vite or a framework default; customize as needed.
items.example: `npm create vite@latest myapp -- --template react-ts` gives you a working TS+React+HMR+production-build setup in 30 seconds. Customise only when you have a concrete need.

items.title: Mind client exposure
items.prefer: Keep secrets server-side; expose only public config.
items.example: `VITE_*` env vars are inlined into client JS — only use for *public* values (analytics ID, public API base URL). Real secrets stay server-side, read in API routes.
